tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via exploration probe (dispatches=4 < 5))

data.summary: Herdr group tabs now refresh short worker-task labels on joins and leaves while cosmetic rename failures are ignored.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
src/drivers/herdr.ts        | 20 ++++++++++++++++++--
 tests/drivers/herdr.test.ts | 32 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 2 files changed, 49 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

✓ a tickmarkr-created group tab is renamed on member join and member leave, label = group name plus at most one hot-state token: renameGroupTab() is called from newGeneration (first join), joinGroup (subsequent join), and close (leave). The label is entry.label plus at most one task-id token derived from the newest live member (`.find(Boolean)` on a single match), falling back to the bare group label when no members remain. The test asserts the exact rename sequence WORKERS · T1 → WORKERS · T2 → WORKERS · T1 → WORKERS across joins and leaves.
✓ tabs not created by tickmarkr are never renamed: renameGroupTab takes a GroupEntry, and GroupEntry instances are only constructed in newGeneration() immediately after tabSlot() creates the tab — there is no adoption path for operator tabs. The test opens an operator-labeled slot (w1:t2) between group joins and the exact-match toEqual on all `tab rename` log lines proves only the driver-owned w1:t1 tab was ever renamed.
✓ rename failures are swallowed (label is cosmetic; never fails a slot or a run): The herdr call is wrapped in try/catch with an explicit '/* cosmetic only */' swallow. The dedicated test stubs `tab rename` to exit 1 and still completes both slot() calls and both close() calls without throwing, asserting all 4 rename attempts were made.
✓ subprocess driver is untouched: The diff touches only src/drivers/herdr.ts and tests/drivers/herdr.test.ts; src/drivers/subprocess.ts is unmodified.

data.provenance: tier cheap (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via prefer)

data.summary: Added tests/drivers/herdr-tab-labels.test.ts: two red-capable regression tests proving join/leave emit short-label tab renames only on the tickmarkr-created group tab, and that arename failure never throws and the slot lifecycle still completes.

data.details:
✓ join then leave emits tab rename commands with short labels for the tickmarkr-created tab only: The first test drives two group-slot joins plus a dedicated role tab and a per-slot tab, then closes both group slots, and asserts the exact recorded rename sequence ['tab rename w1:t1 WORKERS · T1', '... · T2', '... · T1', 'tab rename w1:t1 WORKERS'] — short task-id labels on join/leave transitions. It further asserts the set of renamed tab ids equals exactly {w1:t1} while proving the foreign tabs coexisted (their 'tab create --label' calls are present in the log), so no foreign tab is ever renamed. The incTabs stub gives each tab a distinct id, making the only-the-group-tab claim falsifiable rather than vacuous.
✓ a rename command failure does not throw and the slot lifecycle completes: The second test stubs 'tab rename' as 'exit 1' for every invocation, then awaits both slot() calls (join path) and both close() calls, with `await expect(d.close(s1)).resolves.toBeUndefined()` proving no throw. Lifecycle completion is verified independently of the rename outcome: 4 rename attempts still fired (best-effort) and exactly one 'tab close w1:t1' shows the refcounted teardown reaped the generation's tab despite the failures.
